title: dryopteris affinis subsp. kerryensis
intro: Dryopteris affinis subsp. kerryensis, also known as Kerry male fern, is a subspecies of the Dryopteris affinis fern, native to Ireland and the UK, particularly in the region of Kerry. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Kerry male fern include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve congestion, and soothe respiratory irritations, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the fronds in hot water, although it can also be found in tincture or dried form for herbal preparations.
